name: Fitness Subscription Standard Web2App Funnel
description: Default Web2App skeleton for fitness, strength, weight-loss, Pilates, yoga, calisthenics, walking, senior fitness, and home workout subscription apps. The skeleton is organized by conversion psychology instead of field order.

operatingModel.principle: The onboarding flow is a personalized plan generator and pre-paywall advertorial, not a neutral survey.
sequenceLogic: Use answer-triggered feedback pages to build trust and momentum.
Delay sensitive measurement inputs until the user understands why the plan needs them.
Convert answers into a visible profile summary, analysis, plan generation, and plan-ready bridge before paywall.

feedbackPagePolicy.name: answer_triggered_feedback
feedbackPagePolicy.description: Intro pages should be tied to previous answers or section transitions. They should provide social proof, reassurance, objection handling, or personalization explanation.
feedbackPagePolicy.recommendedCadence: After every 3-6 input pages or immediately after high-intent answers such as age, primary goal, main barrier, and measurements.

stages.name: Entry And Low-Friction Split
stages.goal: Open with a product portal and a low-friction split so the funnel immediately feels personalized.
stages.psychology: Reduce first-step friction and make users self-identify before asking anything sensitive.

slots.purpose: Show product promise, large hero image, start CTA, and login action. The start CTA routes into the first profile question.
Optional first low-friction split when the product has gendered positioning or plan logic. If used, it should appear before age_group.

stages.name: My Profile
stages.goal: Collect broad identity and self-perception signals that make the rest of the funnel feel tailored.
stages.psychology: Let users classify who they are, where they are now, and who they want to become.

slots.purpose: Segment motivation, claims, imagery, and plan tone by life stage. Normally four options.
Show age-relevant social proof or reassurance, similar to 'people like you have already tried this' without copying competitor wording.
Capture how the user describes their current physical build, ability, or comfort level in non-shaming language.
Create current-versus-desired contrast and support later plan ownership.

stages.name: Goal Clarity
stages.goal: Clarify the result the user wants and the body areas or benefits that matter most.
stages.psychology: Turn vague interest into a concrete desired outcome the paywall can later unlock.

slots.purpose: Anchor the rest of the funnel and paywall bridge around the user's main desired outcome.
Explain that the product can build a personalized path toward the selected goal and handle the one-size-fits-all objection.
Let users name specific improvement areas, increasing ownership and giving the plan visible personalization hooks.
Capture what would make the plan feel successful, such as energy, strength, flexibility, confidence, or visible tone.

stages.name: Barriers And Past Attempts
stages.goal: Surface why previous attempts failed and position the product as adaptive rather than generic.
stages.psychology: Reframe failure as a plan-fit problem, not a willpower problem.

slots.purpose: Identify previous attempts so copy can acknowledge lived experience without blame.
Identify the main reason plans have not worked before and later show how the plan adapts around it.
Reassure the user that their barrier is common and solvable through a personalized plan.
Collect physical constraints, pain, discomfort, or support needs when relevant, without making medical claims.

stages.name: Lifestyle Context
stages.goal: Understand everyday context so the plan feels realistic and sustainable.
stages.psychology: Make the plan feel fitted to the user's real life, not an abstract ideal routine.

slots.purpose: Calibrate starting intensity and avoid an intimidating plan.
Match routines to home, gym, chair, mat, outdoor, or no-equipment context.
Make the plan feel achievable by fitting it into available time.
Collect diet, sleep, hydration, stress, or routine signals when relevant to the product category.

stages.name: Body Metrics
stages.goal: Collect measurement data after enough trust has been built.
stages.psychology: Make sensitive inputs feel useful for personalization, not invasive.

slots.purpose: Explain that measurements help set pacing and plan fit. Do not include BMI explanation cards as a UI requirement.
Collect height with real-time unit conversion.
Collect current weight with real-time unit conversion.
Collect goal weight only when weight change is central to the offer.

stages.name: Commitment And Plan Setup
stages.goal: Turn answers into a plan configuration and ask for lightweight commitment.
stages.psychology: Build ownership by letting users choose cadence, intensity, timeline, and readiness.

slots.purpose: Capture expected timeline and set realistic pacing for later plan copy.
Configure weekly cadence and make the plan feel executable.
Let users feel control over the plan difficulty.
Confirm that enough information has been collected to build a personalized plan.

stages.name: Personalization Capture
stages.goal: Capture contact identity when the business wants recovery or plan continuity before the result sequence.
stages.psychology: Ask for email only after users believe a useful plan is being prepared.

stages.name: Result Bridge
stages.goal: Turn answers into proof that a personalized plan exists.
stages.psychology: Shift the user's feeling from 'I answered questions' to 'my plan has been created'.

slots.purpose: Show a concise profile summary based on user answers.
Explain the gap between current state and desired outcome without medical diagnosis or guarantees.
Create anticipation by showing the plan being assembled from profile, goals, barriers, lifestyle, and metrics.
Bridge to paywall by stating the personalized plan is ready and previewing what will be unlocked.

stages.name: Paywall
stages.goal: Convert to subscription after plan ownership has been established.
stages.psychology: The paid offer should feel like unlocking a plan the user already helped create.

slots.purpose: Show personalized result preview, offer, plans, social proof, guarantee, FAQ, legal disclosure, and embedded checkout.

stages.name: Post Purchase Continuation
stages.goal: Confirm unlock, establish account continuity, and expose subscription management.
stages.psychology: Reduce buyer uncertainty and move users toward account/app continuity.
